summary: Give the MCP server the per-call workspace targeting the CLI already has: a top-level "workspace" argument on plane_execute (and defensively on the per-endpoint handler) that overrides the {slug} template var for that one call, matching the CLI precedence --workspace > $PLANE_SLUG > default_workspace.
reason: newMCPClient() bakes the workspace slug from $PLANE_SLUG / default_workspace at config-load time, with no MCP equivalent of the CLI's --workspace flag. When a process-wide PLANE_SLUG is exported in the environment (a common setup for users who switch workspaces per-shell), every MCP request silently targets that one workspace regardless of the agent's intent, surfacing as a confusing HTTP 403 against the workspace the agent actually wanted. This patch restores the missing axis so an agent can retarget a single call. Builds on slug-env-align (the {slug}-from-PLANE_SLUG resolution this overrides) and novel-workspaces (the CLI-side --workspace flag this mirrors).
patch_tags: PATCH(mcp-workspace) in internal/config/config.go: exported NormalizeWorkspaceSlug wrapper over the unexported normalizeWorkspaceSlug so the mcp package reuses the identical bare-slug/browser-URL/API-base normalization.

PATCH(mcp-workspace) in internal/mcp/code_orch.go: a `workspace` string input on the plane_execute tool, plus an applyWorkspaceArg(c, args) call right after newMCPClient() in handleCodeOrchExecute (before path/query/body assembly).

PATCH(mcp-workspace) in internal/mcp/tools.go: an applyWorkspaceArg(c, args) call after newMCPClient() in makeAPIHandler, so the per-endpoint orchestration mode honors the same arg and never leaks it into the query string / body.
validated_outcome: go build ./... + go test ./internal/mcp/ ./internal/config/ green. applyWorkspaceArg overrides TemplateVars["slug"] for bare slug / trailing slash / browser URL / API-base-prefix inputs, is a no-op (config-resolved slug preserved) when the arg is absent, blank, or non-string, consumes the arg in every case so it never reaches the query string or request body, and is nil-safe for both client and args.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Advertise the `workspace` input on the generated per-endpoint MCP tools (not just plane_execute).
deferred_to_upstream.reason: The per-endpoint handler already honors and strips the arg via applyWorkspaceArg, but each generated tool's input schema would need the generator to inject the shared `workspace` string param. Out of scope for a hand-patch; the active code-orchestration surface (plane_execute) advertises it. Candidate for an upstream generator feature so workspace targeting is declared uniformly across orchestration modes.
